name: Kiawah Island (Ocean Course)
address: 1000 Ocean Course Drive, Kiawah Island, SC 29455
green_fee_notes: Peak-season green fees run roughly $600 per round with a walking caddie included in the rate but not gratuity; the resort does not publish exact rates online and quotes them by phone.
notable: Pete and Alice Dye design, opened 1991; ten holes hug the Atlantic, billed as the most seaside holes in the Northern Hemisphere
Host of the 1991 Ryder Cup, the famous 'War by the Shore'
Host of the 2012 (Rory McIlroy) and 2021 (Phil Mickelson) PGA Championships
Named toughest course in America by Golf Digest (2010); the Championship tees carry a slope of 155, the maximum on record
walkable_note: Walking-only with a caddie most of the year; carts permitted only after 10 a.m. in June, July, and August.

confidence.rating_tee: Headline uses the Black tee (77.4/153), the top tee printed on the public scorecard with per-hole yardages. The USGA-only Championship tee is 79.1/155 at 7,876 yards (2021 PGA setup) and appears in NCRDB CourseID 8064 without per-hole data.
confidence.green_fee_notes: Resort publishes no rates online; ~$600 peak figure from 2026 third-party guides.
confidence.tees: Men's USGA values, identical on the official 2025 scorecard and NCRDB. Blue/Red combo omitted from the directory card.
confidence.lat_lng: Course-site coordinates from Wikipedia's 2021 PGA Championship article.
